pi_items.verified_value: blobs.gif is a real, publicly available built-in sample image from Fiji/ImageJ distributed at https://imagej.net/images/blobs.gif
pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified from official URL; confirmed as standard ImageJ sample image through web search and GitHub references

pi_items.verified_value: With proper shading correction and appropriate thresholding, particle counts of 55-65 are achievable, confirming >50 is realistic for corrected images
pi_items.source: Python-based particle analysis of blobs.gif using scipy.ndimage with multiple threshold values; Otsu auto-threshold on properly corrected image yields ~58-64 particles

pi_items.verified_value: Without correction, using threshold ~50 on inverted blobs.gif yields ~39 particles (>50px each); standard auto-threshold methods on uncorrected image produce similar counts
pi_items.source: Python-based particle analysis on blobs.gif; threshold 50 on inverted image gives 39 large components

privileged_info_summary: The blobs.gif dataset is a real, publicly available ImageJ/Fiji built-in sample image (256×254 pixels, 25.2 KB GIF format) accessible from https://imagej.net/images/blobs.gif. This is NOT a synthetic image but a standard scientific image used for testing microscopy image analysis workflows. Particle count claims are verified: uncorrected images with standard thresholding yield approximately 40 particles, while properly corrected images with shading correction yield 55-65 particles, confirming the expected minimum of 50 for successful correction. Output paths reference standard Fiji data directories created during task initialization.
